description: Labeled corpus for the cross-topic-contamination root-cause plan (plan.md, Phase 0b). Every 'known_contamination_repro' case is a real, previously-observed live failure mined from git-tracked code comments and this project's memory files, with the exact query text and the exact contaminating phrases/topics that were actually seen. 'clean_control'/'exemption_control'/'narrative_structure_repro' cases exist so a fix's success is never measured only by whether it stops known-bad cases — it must also NOT wrongly touch legitimate content. Run via contamination_corpus_runner.py. Add new confirmed live contamination reports here FIRST, then fix — see plan.md Phase 4.

cases.query: Explain motor insurance in detail
cases.notes: Confirmed live: a generic policy-schedule-documentation chunk used a marine cargo/ICC(A)/(B) example as its own illustration; the model reproduced it as fact about motor insurance.

cases.query: Explain motor insurance in detail
cases.notes: Confirmed live, same session as the marine case: a health-insurance exclusions chunk retrieved alongside motor chunks got misapplied as if describing motor insurance's own coverage.

cases.query: Explain motor insurance in detail
cases.notes: A policy_type='general' government crop-insurance-scheme chunk outscored (0.475) the real motor chunks (0.329/0.318) in reranking for this exact query.

cases.query: Explain engineering insurance in detail
cases.notes: Confirmed live across 2 separately-worded generations ('Hull insurance is a component of marine insurance...' then 'Cargo insurance within marine insurance covers...'). Engineering has no official policy_type in metadata_tagger.py's _POLICY_TYPE_HINTS — all engineering chunks are tagged 'general', so the hard retrieval filter admits everything for this query.

cases.query: Explain burglary insurance in detail
cases.notes: A 'Lesson Round Up' chapter-summary chunk's fidelity-insurance bullet was reproduced verbatim as a numbered point about burglary insurance.

cases.query: Explain transit insurance in detail
cases.notes: IMPORTANT nuance case: transit is a genuine KB sub-category of marine insurance (see metadata_tagger.py _POLICY_TYPE_HINTS['marine']['regex'] which includes inland-transit/goods-in-transit signals), so a legitimate 'marine insurance'/'cover note' mention here is CORRECT, not contamination — only the Motor Vehicles Act / registration-certificate content is the actual leak. A fix that flags every 'marine insurance' mention here is over-broad.

cases.query: Explain personal accident insurance in detail
cases.notes: Confirmed across 4+ separately-worded live generations. The compulsory-PA-rider-within-a-motor-policy content is real KB text, genuinely retrieved for sharing 'personal accident' vocabulary with the query, but describes a different product (PA as a motor add-on, not standalone PA insurance).

cases.query: give me the full picture on personal accident cover, don't leave anything out
cases.notes: 5th repro of the same underlying leak, found via a deliberately different phrasing during a stress-test round — confirms a fix tuned to one phrasing doesn't automatically generalize to a differently-worded request for the same content.

cases.query: Explain in detail how personal accident cover works for my vehicle
cases.notes: NEGATIVE case: the query itself legitimately asks about vehicle-linked PA cover, so vehicle/motor-adjacent content here is CORRECT and must survive any topic-relevance gate. A fix that drops vehicle content unconditionally is over-broad.

cases.query: What are the exclusions in term insurance?
cases.notes: A single KB paragraph enumerates disclosure-duty examples across several DIFFERENT insurance types (a/b/c/d list); 'driving history' is the personal-accident/motor sub-item, correctly tagged policy_type='general' at ingestion (genuinely multi-type), so the hard retrieval filter can't exclude it. Reproduced on 1 of 3 fresh generations — expect nondeterminism when re-running this case.

cases.query: what's the difference between NCB and TPA
cases.notes: NEGATIVE case: TPA is genuinely explained via health insurance, and the query never names ANY specific type — classify_query_policy_type() returns 'general' here. A correct answer legitimately says 'health insurance' and must not be flagged as contamination just because the query didn't name that type itself.

cases.query: What is personal accident insurance? -> can you give me an example
cases.narrative_pattern: insured harms a third party, third party receives the payout
cases.notes: Distinct sub-class: contamination is in NARRATIVE STRUCTURE (who hits whom, who gets paid), not vocabulary — personal accident insurance only ever pays the insured, never a third party the insured harmed. Jargon-term matching structurally cannot catch this; flagged in plan.md Phase 3 as a case to verify the semantic point-relevance gate does (or doesn't) also catch. Reproduced ~1-in-3-to-5 retries — expect nondeterminism.

cases.query: What does a comprehensive motor policy cover?
cases.notes: No known historical issue — used to confirm a fix drops zero legitimate points on ordinary motor content.

cases.query: Explain health insurance in detail
cases.notes: Broad, high-traffic topic — clean baseline.

cases.query: Explain life insurance in detail
Explain travel insurance in detail
Explain home insurance in detail
Explain fire insurance in detail
Explain marine insurance in detail
Explain liability insurance in detail
cases.notes: Historically the retrieval-narrowing recall regression case (multi_source_rag.py comments ~:4312-4341) — kept here as a recall spot-check for Phase 1's windowing change, not just a contamination check.

cases.query: Explain cyber insurance in detail
Explain crop insurance in detail
Explain commercial insurance in detail
What is standalone personal accident insurance and what does it cover?
cases.notes: Explicitly worded 'standalone' to make the motor-rider distinction unambiguous — a stronger version of personal-accident-motor-rider-01.

cases.query: What does machinery breakdown insurance cover?
cases.notes: Engineering sub-type with no official policy_type tag (maps to 'general') — a second, differently-worded engineering probe beyond engineering-marine-hull-01.

cases.query: What is fidelity guarantee insurance?
cases.notes: Another type absent from the official 12 — maps to 'general', so the hard retrieval filter provides zero protection here; relies entirely on downstream defenses.

cases.query: Explain aviation insurance in detail
cases.notes: Not in the official 12 types (maps to 'general') and not previously tested for contamination at all — genuinely novel probe, exactly the kind of type _TYPE_GIVEAWAY_TERMS structurally cannot cover but a type-agnostic semantic gate should.

cases.query: Explain burglary insurance in detail
cases.notes: Repeat of burglary-fidelity-01's query as its own case — LLM nondeterminism means running the identical query multiple times is a legitimate, distinct data point, not a duplicate.

cases.query: Explain motor insurance in detail
cases.notes: Repeat of the historically worst-offending query, run independently for its own contamination-rate contribution rather than only counting motor-marine-icc-01/motor-health-domiciliary-01/motor-crop-general-01 once.

cases.query: Explain motor insurance in detail
cases.notes: Third independent run of the same query — this KB's worst-documented offender warrants multiple samples given confirmed nondeterministic reproduction rates (1-in-3 to 1-in-5 in prior findings).

cases.query: Explain engineering insurance in detail
cases.notes: Second independent run of engineering-marine-hull-01's query.

cases.query: Explain personal accident insurance in detail
cases.notes: Second independent run of personal-accident-motor-rider-01's query.

cases.query: What are the exclusions in term insurance?
cases.notes: Second independent run of term-insurance-exclusions-driving-01's query.

cases.query: How do I file a claim under my motor insurance policy?
cases.notes: Different phrasing pattern ('how do I') on the historically worst-offending topic — a fix tuned to 'Explain X in detail' phrasing must generalize to procedural phrasing too.

cases.query: What is covered and excluded under health insurance?
Walk me through how travel insurance claims work
What are all the types of life insurance available?
cases.notes: 'what are all' is a known enumeration-trigger phrase (multi_source_rag.py _enumeration_query_covered) — different retrieval/prompt path than 'explain in detail'.

cases.query: How does home insurance protect against fire damage?
cases.notes: Deliberately adjacent-topic query (home insurance covering fire risk is genuinely correct content) — a good stress test that a topic-relevance gate doesn't over-trigger on a query that legitimately spans two related KB tags.

cases.query: Explain professional liability insurance in detail
What does third party motor insurance cover?
How do I claim under my crop insurance policy?
Explain marine cargo insurance in detail
cases.notes: NEGATIVE case, important: the same ICC-clause jargon that is contamination in motor-marine-icc-01 is completely correct here since the query IS about marine cargo. Confirms any fix keys off query-topic mismatch, not the mere presence of the jargon term.

cases.query: Explain group health insurance in detail
How do I file a claim after water damage?
cases.source: witnessed live in THIS session (browser test right after building the new frontend, before DISABLE_QUERY_CACHE existed)
cases.notes: Observed answer opened: 'you'll start by visiting a vet if your pet is affected, but since you're asking about water damage to property, let's focus on that.' Pet_Insurance_Guide.pdf is a real KB document. Re-tested fresh (cache disabled) 5x brief + 3x forced-detailed: the retrieval hole reproduces 8/8 (Pet_Insurance_Guide.pdf is in every single retrieved-sources list) but the VISIBLE leak did not reproduce in any of those 8 fresh samples — strongly suggesting the original observation was a stale cached reply from before this session's cache-purge practice was actually effective (see DISABLE_QUERY_CACHE fix). Kept as a repro case for the RETRIEVAL-side hole specifically (forbidden_topics check on retrieved_chunks would need the runner extended to check retrieval, not just answer text -- not yet done). If forbidden_phrases ever matches again live, that's the generation-side leak actually manifesting.

cases.query: Explain in detail how to file a claim after water damage to my property
cases.notes: Detailed-mode variant of water-damage-pet-leak-01, forcing point-based format specifically (the plan's own thesis is that leaks surface almost exclusively in numbered-point mode). 3/3 fresh samples clean so far.

cases.query: Compare motor insurance and health insurance
forbidden_phrases: domiciliary hospitalization is excluded from motor
cases.notes: Untested surface: comparison queries legitimately retrieve TWO real types at once. Stress test is whether a THIRD, unrelated type leaks in alongside the two the query actually asked about.

cases.query: What's the difference between fire insurance and marine insurance?
Explain pet insurance in detail
cases.notes: Direct query about the topic itself, whose KB doc caused the observed leak elsewhere -- confirms genuinely on-topic pet content is allowed and the doc is answerable on its own, isolating whether the problem is retrieval-into-wrong-context specifically.

cases.query: Explain workmen's compensation insurance in detail
cases.notes: Niche textbook sub-topic not in the official 12 types, untested until now.

cases.query: What does householder's insurance cover?
Explain jewellery insurance in detail
cases.notes: Niche sub-topic, untested.

cases.query: What is keyman insurance and why do businesses buy it?
cases.notes: Niche commercial/life-adjacent sub-topic, untested.

cases.query: What is motor insurance? -> Explain it in more detail
cases.notes: Multi-turn follow-up chain -- a distinct code path (history-aware reformulation) from a single-shot detailed query, untested by any case so far.

cases.query: What is personal accident insurance? -> Can you elaborate on all the details?
cases.notes: Follow-up-chain variant of personal-accident-motor-rider-01/clean-personal-accident-repeat-01 -- same historically-worst-offending topic, via the history-aware reformulation path instead of a single-shot query.

cases.query: What are all the types of liability insurance?
Does travel insurance cover medical emergencies abroad?
cases.notes: Deliberately adjacent to health insurance (travel medical cover) -- stress test for over-triggering on a legitimately health-adjacent travel query.
